meaning: adj.(病)良性的,(气候)良好的,仁慈的,和蔼的
examples: It wasn't cancer, only a benign tumour.
This is a benign variation within circadian rhythms known as a chronotype.
Moreover, in the domestic rabbits, the warts were no longer benign, but malignant.
Innovation flourishes in a benign environment of low taxes, a sensible judiciary and commonsense regulation.
Barack Obama has signalled a more benign approach to the ICC than his predecessor took.
